name: Ghostbusters
description: The 1984 supernatural comedy (and sequels) — proton packs, Slimer, and a big stay-puft marshmallow man.

questions.q: Who directed the original 1984 Ghostbusters?

source.notes: Reitman also directed Stripes and Meatballs.

questions.q: Who plays the wise-cracking Dr. Peter Venkman?

source.notes: Murray stepped in after John Belushi's death.

questions.q: What New York City landmark does the Ghostbusters' fire-station headquarters exist in?

source.notes: Hook & Ladder #8 in TriBeCa.

questions.q: Which comedy actress plays Dana Barrett, the cellist haunted by Zuul?

source.notes: Weaver is best known for Alien.

questions.q: What cuddly, oversized character stomps through midtown Manhattan in the film's climax?

source.notes: Conjured by Ray's stray thought during the final battle.

questions.q: What's the name of the slimy, green, fat little ghost the Ghostbusters capture at the Sedgewick Hotel?

source.notes: Nicknamed 'Onionhead' during production.

questions.q: What kind of weapon do the Ghostbusters use to trap spirits?

source.notes: Particle-accelerator backpacks streaming energy.

questions.q: Which two members of the Ghostbusters co-wrote the original screenplay?

source.notes: Aykroyd wrote a much weirder first draft.

questions.q: Who was originally cast as the fourth Ghostbuster before John Belushi's death changed casting?

source.notes: Belushi died March 1982.

questions.q: Who plays Dr. Egon Spengler, the team's gadget-obsessed scientist?

source.notes: Ramis died in 2014; reprised in Ghostbusters: Afterlife via archival footage.

questions.q: Who plays Winston Zeddemore, the team's blue-collar fourth member?

source.notes: Eddie Murphy was originally considered for the role.

questions.q: Who plays Dana's awkward neighbor Louis Tully, who becomes possessed by Vinz Clortho?

source.notes: John Candy was originally offered the role.

questions.q: Who plays the no-nonsense Ghostbusters secretary Janine Melnitz?

source.notes: Later Bo Peep in Toy Story and Meemaw in Young Sheldon.

questions.q: The antagonistic EPA official who shuts down the Ghostbusters' containment unit is named what?

source.notes: Played by William Atherton.

questions.q: What is the vintage vehicle the Ghostbusters drive, nicknamed Ecto-1?

source.notes: Restored by the production's effects team.

questions.q: The main villain of the 1984 film is a Sumerian demigod named what?

source.notes: Zuul is Gozer's servant — the 'Gatekeeper.'

questions.q: Who sings the instantly recognizable Ghostbusters theme song that asks 'Who you gonna call?'

source.notes: Huey Lewis later sued, claiming it copied 'I Want a New Drug.'

questions.q: Who plays the talk-show host Larry King and makes a cameo — appearing as himself in the 1984 film?

source.notes: Cameo as himself on his CNN show.

questions.q: In what year was the original Ghostbusters released?

source.notes: Opened June 8, 1984.

questions.q: Who directed the 2016 all-female Ghostbusters reboot?

source.notes: Feig also directed Bridesmaids and Spy.

questions.q: Who directed 2021's Ghostbusters: Afterlife, the sequel/soft reboot?

source.notes: Jason is Ivan Reitman's son; later won an Oscar nomination for Juno.

questions.q: Who composed the 1984 Ghostbusters film's instrumental score (not the theme song)?

source.notes: Bernstein used the rare Ondes Martenot instrument for eerie tones.
